test(simulator): add split-domain EL3202 recovery scenario

Summary:
- add scenario 37 for the captured EL3202 reconnect PREOP timeout with
  digital loopback on a separate PDO domain
- update the simulator README catalog and next-directions text to mark
  the split-domain captured-device follow-up as covered

Rationale:
- continue the numbered simulator loop from test/integration/simulator/README.md
  with the next concrete captured-device regression after scenario 36
- pin the domain split explicitly so the suite proves RTD recovery can
  degrade its own domain without falsely degrading unrelated loopback I/O

Validation:
- mix test test/integration/simulator/37_split_domain_el3202_reconnect_preop_timeout_test.exs
- mix test test/integration/simulator
- Found by:
  test/integration/simulator/37_split_domain_el3202_reconnect_preop_timeout_test.exs

Co-authored-by: Codex <codex@openai.com>

test(simulator): fold transport baseline into scenario loop

Summary:
- add 00_healthy_ring_transport_matrix.{md,test.exs} for the
  transport-aware healthy ring baseline across UDP, raw, and
  redundant raw
- remove the ad hoc ring_test.exs and
  redundant_raw_ring_test.exs coverage and point the README at the
  numbered scenario path

Rationale:
- keep simulator transport coverage aligned with the cyclic scenario
  workflow documented in test/integration/simulator/README.md
- make UDP assertions explicit so the baseline still exercises UDP
  even when ETHERCAT_INTEGRATION_TRANSPORT defaults helper scenarios
  to raw

Validation:
- mix test test/integration/simulator/00_healthy_ring_transport_matrix_test.exs
- mix test test/integration/simulator
- Scenario:
  test/integration/simulator/00_healthy_ring_transport_matrix_test.exs

Add shareable URL state for calculator inputs (#12)

* feat(calculator): add shareable URL state

Summary:
- add strict calculator state serialization and restoration helpers for
  query-string deep links
- initialize the calculator from the URL and keep the current URL synced
  with valid calculator scenarios via replaceState
- cover valid restore, convert-source restore, malformed fallback, and
  clean default URLs in automated tests

Rationale:
- runners need a copyable link that reopens the same valid calculator
  setup without re-entering values
- strict parsing keeps malformed or contradictory query state from
  breaking the calculator and keeps empty or invalid edits off the URL

Tests:
- npm test
- npm run build
- npm run check

Co-authored-by: Codex <codex@openai.com>

* chore(workflow): align PR publish metadata for HEL-17

Summary:
- update the stored PR draft to match the HEL-17 URL-state change and
  current validation evidence
- add a repo-local PR title file for the current issue
- teach the PR publish helper to read a title override from env or the
  repo-local title file instead of hard-coding the starter issue title

Rationale:
- the offline handoff should stay self-contained and accurate when GitHub
  publishing has to be retried outside this environment
- the previous publish helper would have opened a mismatched PR title for
  this branch even after auth and network were restored

Tests:
- bash -n scripts/create_pr.sh
- npm run pr:dry-run

Co-authored-by: Codex <codex@openai.com>

* test(app): cover URL restore and sync behavior

Summary:
- add a no-dependency app-level test harness that exercises main.js URL
  restore, URL syncing, and malformed-query cleanup behavior
- verify valid edits update history.replaceState without reloading the page
- document the shipped deep-link behavior in the README

Rationale:
- the existing library tests proved serialization logic but not the DOM
  wiring that restores from location.search and syncs the URL after edits
- README should mention the new shareable-link behavior now that it ships

Tests:
- node --test tests/url-state.test.js
- npm run check

Co-authored-by: Codex <codex@openai.com>

* fix(calculator): keep empty state out of share URLs

Summary:
- stop empty finish or convert states from being treated as current
  results in the calculator view layer
- add regression coverage for empty finish-mode serialization and the
  reset-to-clean-URL path in the app-level URL-state test
- preserve the shareable URL behavior for valid scenarios while making
  reset and malformed fallback truly return to a clean URL

Rationale:
- the new app-level reset test exposed that empty inputs in finish mode
  were still being serialized into query params after reset
- valid deep links should be shareable, but blank calculator state should
  never produce a broken or contradictory URL

Tests:
- node --test tests/url-state.test.js
- node --test tests/calculator.test.js
- npm run check

Co-authored-by: Codex <codex@openai.com>

* chore(workflow): sync HEL-17 publish notes with branch name

Summary:
- update the HEL-17 PR draft publish notes to use the Linear-aligned
  branch name
- point the fallback import command at the canonical handoff bundle path

Rationale:
- keep the checked-in publish guidance consistent with the branch metadata
  used by Linear and the refreshed handoff manifest
- reduce ambiguity for the eventual networked publish step while GitHub
  access is still blocked in this workspace

Tests:
- npm run pr:dry-run

Co-authored-by: Codex <codex@openai.com>

* fix(workflow): drop stale handoff bundles on refresh

Summary:
- remove old repo bundle files when prepare-handoff rewrites an existing
  output directory
- add a regression test for rerunning handoff generation after a branch
  name change

Rationale:
- keep the HEL-17 handoff directory canonical after the branch-alignment
  cleanup work in this session
- avoid leaving multiple conflicting bundle names behind for the same
  issue handoff package

Tests:
- node --test tests/handoff.test.js
- npm run check

Co-authored-by: Codex <codex@openai.com>

Fix team page project visibility (#231)

* chore(repo): remove shared symphony workflow files

Summary:
- remove the repo-level WORKFLOW.md used only for local Symphony runs
- remove the copied .codex skills from the shared bug-board repo
- keep the repo clean for contributors who are not using Symphony

Rationale:
- Symphony now uses a machine-local workflow outside the shared repo
- temporary issue workspaces still receive the needed skills during setup
- this avoids leaving local automation files in a shared codebase

Tests:
- not run (repo metadata and workflow file removal only)

Co-authored-by: Codex <codex@openai.com>

feat: add admin entitlements and shared project improvements

- backend:
  - add admin email allowlist parsing, user-email auth payloads, and a me/entitlements endpoint
  - enforce admin unlimited quota by bypassing exhaustion checks and skipping post-run usage increments
  - keep websocket generation/chat flow aligned with authenticated user context and start-generation args
- frontend:
  - add shared project client flow for /p/[slug] with owner/public modes and read-only behavior
  - add admin entitlement fetch and show unlimited quota labels in dashboard, top bar, and questionnaire
  - preserve chat streaming and pipeline UX while integrating owner/public and quota-state controls
- database:
  - add migrations to make projects public by default and remove client profile self-update policy
- tests:
  - add admin allowlist and generation-service admin quota tests
  - extend generation-start and websocket tests for auth user payloads and entitlements behavior

Closes #17
Closes #19
Closes #31

Co-Authored-By: codex <codex@openai.com>

fix(codex): enforce completion comments via dynamic tools

Summary:
- add a typed Linear issue comment path and require a completion
  comment before a Codex turn is accepted
- advertise and handle dynamic Linear tools using the current
  app-server request and response schema
- update workflow guidance and regression tests for the completion
  comment contract

Rationale:
- prompt-only guidance was not enough to guarantee that completed
  tickets received a Linear summary comment
- the real Codex app-server protocol differs from the earlier fake
  test shape, so the runtime needed schema-compliant dynamic tool
  requests and responses

Tests:
- npm test -- test/codex/app-server.test.ts test/codex/agent-runner.test.ts
- npm run build

Co-authored-by: Codex <codex@openai.com>
